finding: May be associated with: hypercoagulable state, CHF, vasculitis

differentials.name: precipitated by coughing, straining, overeating, marathon running
differentials.raw: precipitated by coughing, straining, overeating, marathon running

differentials.name: SECONDARY OMENTAL INFARCTION (more common) Cause:   postoperative omental adhesion, omental mass, vascular damage related to trauma / inflammation / hernia
differentials.raw: SECONDARY OMENTAL INFARCTION (more common) Cause:   postoperative omental adhesion, omental mass, vascular damage related to trauma / inflammation / hernia

differentials.name: subacute RLQ / RUQ abdominal pain of a few days duration
differentials.raw: subacute RLQ / RUQ abdominal pain of a few days duration

differentials.raw: Dx:   clinically difficult; often 1st suggested by radiologist

context: Path:   hemorrhagic infarction ← vascular compromise ← (a) tenuous blood supply to right edge of omentum, (b) kinking of veins Age:   pediatric patients Location:   commonly RLQ on inferior right side of omentum Age:   adult, M > F Site:   right inferior portion of omentum > left Size:   diameter > 5 cm CT: √  solitary encapsulated triangular / oval fatty mass: √  soft-tissue stranding adjacent to ascending colon = haziness of fat anterior to colon (early / mild infarction) √  internal heterogeneity ← soft-tissue stranding adjacent to ascending colon √  whorled pattern of concentric linear fat
